what_it_does: This file stores metadata for a single channel attachment: it records an id, the design-assets channel, an images attachment type, a target image URI, and an added timestamp.
observations: The entire payload is YAML frontmatter with no Markdown body.
The attachment id is att-0085215e and the channel is design-assets.
attachment_type is images and the target is image://design-assets/icons/image (27).
There is no descriptive text, alt text, or asset dimensions.
standouts: Target URI contains literal spaces and parentheses: 'image (27)'.
It is a ledger-style attachment record rather than the image file itself.
Channel is design-assets, indicating a design asset registry.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
File name att-<id>.md matches the id field.
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit timezone offset.
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme scoped to a channel path.
Breaks typical URI conventions by leaving spaces and parentheses unescaped.
concerns: Target string contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may break URI parsers or link resolution.
No checksum, MIME type, or file extension to verify the referenced asset.
No alt text, title, or context describing the image's meaning or usage.
The record does not state whether the target URI is resolvable or canonical.
notes: A bare metadata stub that catalogs an image attachment for the design-assets channel.
questions: Is image://design-assets/icons/image (27) a resolvable URI or a display label?
Where is the actual image file stored and what is its format?
What does the number '(27)' indicate—version, index, or arbitrary filename?
Is this attachment associated with a specific message or design artifact in the channel?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's name and fields identify it as a channel attachment record; it does exactly that by storing the attachment metadata, so purpose and actual content align.
intention_signals: To create a discoverable, timestamped ledger entry that links the design-assets channel to a specific image asset for later retrieval or reference.
novelty: The target URI includes literal spaces and parentheses instead of percent-encoded characters, which is unusual for a reference identifier.
contribution: It contributes a structured, addressable attachment record that maps a channel, attachment type, target URI, and creation timestamp, supporting asset discovery and audit trails without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment ledger record with id att-0085215e for the design-assets channel, typed as images, targeting image://design-assets/icons/image (27), added on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To register and timestamp an image asset reference in the design-assets channel so it can be located as a channel attachment.
